title: hebe chathamica
intro: Hebe chathamica, also known as Chatham Islands hebe, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to the Chatham Islands in New Zealand and is a shrub that grows up to 1 meter tall, with bell-shaped flowers that are purple or pink in color. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and stems of Hebe chathamica can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have health benefits including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health.
